title: vicia cretica subsp. cretica
intro: Vicia cretica subsp. cretica, also known as Cretan vetch, is a plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health issues such as fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. The plant's leaves and stems contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to provide numerous health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Vicia cretica subsp. cretica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
